linux-terminal-fundamentals.title: Linux Terminal Fundamentals
linux-terminal-fundamentals.description: A practical first Linux course for moving around the terminal, organizing files, reading text, and finishing a real workspace cleanup.
linux-terminal-fundamentals.moreComingSoon: More Linux lessons are coming soon.

linux-1-terminal-navigation.title: Terminal Navigation Basics
linux-1-terminal-navigation.description: Get comfortable with the prompt, your current folder, file lists, and safe movement through a workspace.
outcomes: Explain what a terminal command does.
Use pwd and ls to inspect the current location.
Use cd, ., .., and ~ to move around safely.
Create a simple folder map using terminal commands.
why: Every terminal task starts with knowing where you are.
Small, visible changes build confidence before scripts, Git, SSH, or servers.

linux-2-files-and-folders.title: Files, Folders, and Viewing Content
linux-2-files-and-folders.description: Create useful folder structures, clean up files, and read text without leaving the terminal.
outcomes: Create folders and files with mkdir, mkdir -p, and touch.
Copy, move, and rename files using cp and mv.
Use safe deletion habits with rm.
Inspect text files with cat, head, tail, and wc.
why: File organization is a daily skill for development and server work.
Reading and cleaning files from the terminal makes larger tasks less intimidating.

linux-3-file-room-capstone.title: Final Capstone: File Room Handoff
linux-3-file-room-capstone.description: Clean up a messy event workspace and leave it ready for the next person.
outcomes: Inspect a messy workspace before changing it.
Create a clean folder structure for a real task.
Move, rename, copy, and remove files safely.
Use file-reading commands to check your work before finishing.
why: Brings the first course together in one realistic terminal job.
Builds the habit of checking, organizing, and handing off work clearly.

linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-1-orie.title: Terminal Orientation
linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-1-orie.description: Start with the terminal prompt, current directory, listing files, and moving between folders.

linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-1-modu.title: Module Project
linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-1-modu.description: Build a small field kit map with clear folders and check files.
bullets: Place files in the right folders
Verify structure with ls and pwd

linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-2-file.title: File Workflow
linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-2-file.description: Practice the commands developers use every day to build and organize project folders.

linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-2-modu.title: Module Project
linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-2-modu.description: Clean a downloaded school folder into notes, assignments, scripts, and backups.

linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-3-caps.title: Capstone Project
linux-terminal-fundamentals-linux-3-caps.description: Take a messy event folder and leave behind a clean handoff.
bullets: Move and back up important files
